description_long: This sculpture depicts a headless and armless human figure, draped in classical robes, standing on a rectangular plinth. The figure's left leg is slightly forward, and the drapery is intricately carved, suggesting movement and the weight of the fabric. The right arm is missing, while the left arm is partially preserved and appears to be holding or resting against the drapery. The base is solid and blocky, with a step-like feature on one side. The surface is weathered, with visible chisel marks and a matte, stone-like finish. The overall appearance suggests an ancient or classical style, reminiscent of Greco-Roman statuary.
description: A weathered, headless stone sculpture of a draped human figure stands on a rectangular plinth, with detailed folds in the robe and missing limbs.
description_view_0: Front view shows the torso, draped robes, and both legs, with the left arm partially preserved and the right arm missing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the depth of the drapery and the blocky base, with the figure's profile and robe folds visible.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the figure and the plinth, with smooth, less detailed surfaces and the absence of the head and arms.
description_view_3: Right side view shows the curvature of the back and the drapery, as well as the solid, rectangular base.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
